title: Lazy Loading Images
description:
Implement lazy loading for images using Intersection Observer so images only load when they scroll into view.

### Requirements

- Images below the fold should NOT load until they scroll near the viewport
- Use `IntersectionObserver` with `rootMargin: '200px'` for eager pre-loading
- Show a placeholder skeleton while the image hasn't loaded yet
- Once the image enters the viewport, set the `src` attribute to trigger loading
- After the image loads, hide the skeleton and show the image with a fade-in
- Add `loading="lazy"` as a progressive enhancement
- Clean up the observer on unmount

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Image container | `img-container-{index}` |
| Image element | `img-{index}` |
| Skeleton | `skeleton-{index}` |
| Images loaded count | `loaded-count` |
constraints:
- Must use IntersectionObserver API
- Images must not have src set until observed
- Skeleton must show until image fully loads
- Observer must be disconnected on unmount
- Must work with variable-height images
hints: Use a data-src attribute or store the URL in state. Only copy it to src when the observer fires.
Use the image's `onLoad` event to detect when it's fully loaded and hide the skeleton.
Create one IntersectionObserver and observe all image elements. Use entry.target to identify which image was intersected.
